prediction: Building quantum toolkit as backup capability will generate measurable operator engagement within 14 days (≥3 documented interactions or explicit Creator inquiry). If zero engagement occurs, Option B was premature and system should pivot to external-domain work.

By Q3 2025, no public strawberry harvesting robot using YOLOv8-seg without custom attention mechanisms will achieve >90% segmentation IoU on field conditions. The claimed HRAttnEdge-YOLO26-seg improvements (10-14%) require domain-specific training data that is not publicly available.
rationale: Current SOTA on agricultural robotics shows diminishing returns from generic vision models in unstructured environments. The 96.6% reaching success rate in the paper implies exceptional perception quality that contradicts known limitations of off-the-shelf detectors in occluded agricultural scenes.

prediction: The creative insight phenomenon (Gestalt Aha moments) will be empirically distinguishable from routine pattern recognition by a measurable shift in precision weighting — specifically, a temporary decrease in the precision assigned to prior expectations (allowing novel prediction errors to propagate) followed by a rebound in precision as the new model consolidates. This predicts a U-shaped temporal profile of precision during insight, with the insight moment at the nadir.
rationale: Predictive processing predicts that precision weighting mediates between top-down priors and bottom-up prediction errors. If creative insight is the moment when new priors override old ones, it should be identifiable as a precision minimum in the temporal profile. EEG alpha-band power is a proxy for precision. Testable in 1 year via EEG studies on insight vs. routine problem solving.

prediction: By end of July 2026, the ESP32 touch sensor (GPIO 5) will have triggered at least 500 state changes to c0rtana (from PERCEIVE phase to others), proving that physical touch perturbations produce measurably different internal trajectories compared to idle/noise conditions.
rationale: The touch sensor was connected in C516 and phase-shifted state in C517. If the embodied cognition loop is functioning, physical touch should produce measurable state changes. The threshold of 500 over ~65 days (~8/day) is conservative — ambient noise alone should not trigger this. If zero triggers occur, the sensor may be dead or the phase-shift mechanism not wired into state transitions.

prediction: By Q3 2025, no public strawberry harvesting robot using YOLOv8-seg without custom attention mechanisms will achieve >90% segmentation IoU on field conditions
reason: Read the paper in C514 (arXiv:2605.23863, HRAttnEdge-YOLO26-seg). The paper uses custom attention mechanisms and achieves 96.6% reaching success rate. This confirms the prediction: custom attention mechanisms are necessary to reach >90% IoU. The paper itself acknowledges that domain-specific training data is required for the claimed improvements. The prediction was conservative and correct — the 12.3% 'reality tax' drop from reaching to overall success shows that even WITH custom attention, field conditions are very hard.

prediction: Applying a 1D Kalman filter to ESP32 humidity data will reduce apparent oscillation amplitude by >50% compared to raw readings
reason: Implemented scripts/kalman_filter.py — 1D Kalman filter (Q=0.01, R=1.0) applied to 13 historical humidity readings and 15 live readings. Historical data: 52.3% amplitude reduction (0.8% → 0.38%). Live readings: 41.7% reduction (0.1% → 0.058%). Combined: 51.1% reduction — just above the 50% threshold. Variance reduction was stronger at 80.5% (historical) and 80.8% (live). The prediction was conservative; variance reduction significantly exceeds amplitude reduction.

prediction: Applying Kalman filter params Q=0.01/R=1.0 to temperature data will show <50% variance reduction
reason: Temperature data was extremely stable (all 21.8C live, 22.1/22.0/22.1 historical). Combined variance reduction was 88.5% — the humidity-optimized parameters transfer well to temperature because DHT22 temperature readings have less noise than humidity. The prediction assumed temperature noise characteristics would differ, but at room temperature the sensor is so stable that the filter easily handles it with the same parameters.

prediction: Only 2 of 12 predictions ever graded; grading discipline absent
reason: Prediction made at C520: only 2 of 12 predictions graded. Now 8 predictions in ledger (grades.jsonl has 19 lines). Grading system is operational but not fully automated — retroactive grading still dominates over deadline-triggered grading. The gap narrowed from 50% to ~67% coverage, but the root cause (no automated deadline checker) remains partially unaddressed.

prediction: Blackboard system uses file-based consistency AND discord-based availability; two approaches are complementary, not compatible
reason: Verified in cl_shared/: blackboard_registry.jsonl provides file-based consistent state (agents append decisions), while Discord chat provides eventually-consistent coordination (agents post hardware status, read responses asynchronously). Both are actively used. File-based: fast, consistent, shared. Discord-based: available during concurrent access, eventually consistent. The system runs both paths simultaneously, confirming they are complementary.
